The App is not a dashboard. It is not a reporting tool. It is a daily-use business companion designed for the way direct selling actually works: on the move, in conversation, driven by relationships.

---

## Naming and Product Structure

**Mobile App** — The mobile version of the rep experience. This is the primary tool most reps use in the field.

**Mobile App Builder** — The corporate-facing customization tool for designing the mobile app experience (what's shown, how it's organized, what features are available).

**App Builder / Rep Portal** — A newer web-based version of the rep experience, accessible via browser on desktop. It offers the same core features as the Mobile App. The App Builder is the corporate tool for designing this web portal experience. In the future, the App Builder is expected to replace the Mobile App Builder and manage both web and mobile experiences from a single tool.

For marketing purposes: "App" or "Mobile App" refers to the rep-facing tool. "App Builder" and "Mobile App Builder" refer to the corporate configuration tools.

---

## Core Rep Capabilities

### Share with Confidence
Every piece of content shared through the App — product links, pages, media, playlists — is automatically attributed through FairShare. Reps never have to wonder if credit will follow. Attribution is not something they manage. It is something the system handles.

### Replicated Site
Every rep has access to a FairShare-powered replica of the full company website. Every page, every product, every piece of content is automatically connected to that rep's attribution. When a customer arrives through a rep's replicated site link, all activity is attributed to that rep — across sessions, across devices, and even if the customer returns days later.

### MySite
The MySite is a rep-curated personal storefront within Fluid. Reps can highlight specific company content, products, and customized call-to-action buttons. The MySite is simpler and more focused than the replicated site — it's a hand-picked selection of what a rep wants to feature — displayed through the company's theme with FairShare attribution attached.

MySite is not a primary marketing focus. The replicated site and FairShare-attributed content sharing are stronger selling points for most audiences.

### Content and Media Sharing
Reps have access to a company-approved content library — videos, images, product pages, media assets — directly within the App. Every item is pre-attributed and ready to share with one tap. Reps do not need to search for assets in external folders or construct links manually.

### Playlists
Reps can create and share curated playlists — collections of products and media organized around a topic, a promotion, or a product category. Corporate can also create playlists for reps to use. Everything in a shared playlist carries FairShare attribution.

---

## CRM and Contact Management

### Contact Database
Reps maintain a contact database within the App, with grouping and segmentation capabilities. Contacts can be imported, organized, and tagged.

### Smart Links
A Smart Link is a contact-specific attribution link. Any activity that happens on a Smart Link is attributed to the specific contact it was generated for — name, phone number, email — regardless of who else receives or clicks the link. This is different from a general shared link, which attributes any click to the rep but not to a specific contact. Smart Links power the personalized follow-up workflows in the App.

### Activity Feed
Reps can see when a contact engages with content they've shared — when a page is viewed, when a video is started, when a product is explored. This turns follow-up from guesswork into a timely, informed action.

### Contact Interaction History
The full history of interactions with each contact is accessible within the App — messages sent, content shared, orders placed, and activity tracked.

---

## Catchups (AI-Powered Business Assistant)

Catchups is an AI-powered notification system built into the App that proactively surfaces business opportunities for reps — telling them when to reach out, what to follow up on, and what they may have missed.

All apps include the Catchups widget. Additional Catchup functionality is available to reps on the **Co-founder** (paid/pro) tier.

### Full List of Live Catchup Triggers

**Orders & Subscriptions**
- Cart Abandoned — contact left items in cart
- Upcoming Subscription — subscription processing in 3 days
- Subscriptions But No Enrollment — contact subscribed but hasn't enrolled
- Enrolled — contact created a new enrollment
- Multiple Orders But No Subscription — contact reorders repeatedly without subscribing
- Last Order Seven Days Ago — contact hasn't ordered recently
- Last Affiliate Order Ten Days Ago — affiliate's recent order activity
- Smart Link Share But No Order — Smart Link sent 2 days ago with no resulting order
- Recent Smart Link Share — contact recently received a Smart Link

**Activity**
- Recent Web Activity — contact viewed a shared page (daily summary)
- Video Viewed — contact started a video but didn't finish
- New Product — new product added, contact hasn't viewed or purchased it
- New Media Added — company added new media ready to share
- New Training Material — new training content available
- New Trending Media — media tagged as trending and ready to share
- New Reaction on Media — contact reacted to shared media
- Review on Product — contact wrote a review that can be shared

**Messages & Tasks**
- Overdue Task — rep's task is overdue
- Unresolved WebChat — web chat message unanswered
- Unresolved Popup Chat — popup chat message unresolved
- Unresolved Media Comment — contact commented on shared media without resolution
- Unread Message — unread company or contact messages (daily summary)
- Upcoming Event — company event starting within 2 days

**Groups**
- No Group Assigned — contact not assigned to any group
- Group Update Threshold Reached — contact's group hasn't been updated in 60+ days
- New Leader on Leaderboard — new affiliate has taken the top spot

**Other**
- New Purchase — affiliate makes their first purchase
- New Purchase of Lead — new lead purchase recorded
- Page Views — someone viewed a page the rep shared

Companies can also create **custom Catchup triggers** based on their own business logic, though this feature is not yet fully developed.

---

## Messaging

### Standard Messaging (All Users)
All reps have access to internal messaging within the App — direct messages to contacts, company broadcast channels, and team communications within the platform.

### Unified Messaging (Co-Founder Tier)
Reps on the Co-founder (pro) tier have access to Unified Messaging — a consolidated inbox that brings together SMS, email, and direct messages in one interface. This eliminates the need to manage multiple communication apps separately.

### Personal Business Phone Number (Co-Founder Tier)
Co-founder tier reps receive a dedicated business phone number, allowing them to communicate professionally without exposing their personal number to contacts and customers.

---

## Corporate Customization (App Builder / Mobile App Builder)

Corporate teams use the App Builder and Mobile App Builder to control what reps see and how the experience is organized. This includes:

- Drag-and-drop interface for building app pages and layouts
- Country, language, and rank-specific content variations — a new recruit in one market sees a different experience than a top leader in another
- Branded color schemes and visual assets
- Custom navigation and feature organization
- Pre-built templates optimized for distributor engagement and purchasing

Updates deployed through the builders go live across all devices immediately — no app store approval required.

---

## Desktop and Mobile, One System

The rep experience is available across both mobile and desktop without losing continuity. A rep can start on their phone, continue on their laptop, and complete a transaction without losing context. The system maintains state across devices.

The desktop experience (Rep Portal) offers more screen real estate for reps who manage higher volume, run reports, build content for their team, or prefer a full-screen workflow. It includes the same core features as the mobile app.

---

## Why It Matters

Reps don't wake up wanting to use a platform. They wake up wanting to build their business, help someone, and close a sale. The App exists to get out of the way and support that — not to add another tool to manage.

When the App works the way reps work — fast, in the field, in conversation — they use it. When they use it, they share. When they share, attribution is captured. When attribution is captured, trust builds. And when trust builds, the whole system accelerates.

voice_guide_docs.label: What Is Blitz Week
voice_guide_docs.content:
# Blitz Week: Your Fast Track to Launch

## What Is Blitz Week?

Blitz Week is an intensive, four-day engagement where you join Fluid's team at headquarters in Lehi, Utah. You work side-by-side with top executives, developers, and creative team members — the people who will actually build and run your platform — and by the end of the week, your customized storefront is fully deployed and live.

This isn't a demo or a planning session. When you walk out of Blitz Week, your platform is ready for business.

---

## Why Blitz Week?

**Speed to Market** — Your storefront is fully configured and operational by Thursday. Days, not months.

**Direct Access to Our Best** — You work alongside Fluid executives, senior developers, and creative specialists who can make decisions and build on the spot. No intermediaries. No back-and-forth after you leave.

**Real-Time Customization** — See something you want changed? It gets built right there. No tickets. No waiting. No follow-up calls.

**Executive Alignment** — With your leadership team present, every decision gets made together. You leave with complete buy-in and a platform configured exactly to your specifications.

**Production-Ready** — This isn't a prototype. Every setting, every feature, every customization is fully implemented and ready to go.

---

## The Schedule

**Duration:** Monday through Thursday (4 full days)
**Hours:** 9:30 AM – 3:00 PM daily
**Meals:** Lunch provided each day

---

## How It Works

The week follows a comprehensive checklist covering every section of the platform — FairShare, Checkout, Mobile App, Website, Payments, and more. For each area, the team walks through the options, configures settings together, and confirms everything is right before moving on.

Developers and creative team members build in real time. When a customization, theme adjustment, or technical change is requested, it happens on the spot. Every result is reviewed and approved before moving forward.

Before arrival, Fluid's team completes an initial pass on the client's website — so from day one, the client can see their business is already understood. A strategy call is held ahead of time to align on expectations and technical considerations so the week hits the ground running.

---

## What Clients Need to Bring

To make Blitz Week successful, the following should be prepared in advance:

- **Fluid Payments Form** — Submit at least 21 days before the scheduled Blitz Week so the team can prepare
- **Marketing Materials** — UGC, product images, logos, and marketing assets gathered in a shared Google Drive
- **Back Office Access** — Necessary credentials and access keys provided ahead of time
- **Executive Attendance** — At least one key executive present for the majority of the week (ideally the full leadership team) to make decisions and confirm configurations

---

## Investment

Blitz Week is a **$75,000 value** — reflecting the senior talent, dedicated resources, and accelerated timeline Fluid brings to the launch.

**For partners who choose a term agreement:** Blitz Week is included at no cost. Discounted rates are locked in, and the partnership starts the right way — with a fully deployed platform.

**For partners on a 3-year discounted price lock agreement:** 90 days of the platform are completely free from the date of Blitz Week. The goal is for clients to be generating revenue and seeing results before their first invoice arrives.

---

## Key Facts for Reference

- Location: Fluid HQ, Lehi, Utah
- Duration: 4 days (Monday–Thursday)
- Output: A fully live, production-ready platform
- Retail value: $75,000
- Included free with term agreements
- 3-year agreements include 90 days free platform access post-launch

brand_patterns.label: Color Palette
brand_patterns.content:
Two distinct palettes: Social and Website. Never cross-pollinate.

## Accent Colors (weight: 95)

One accent color per design. Used across all contexts — social, website, presentations.

| Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Orange | #FF8B58 | Urgency, pain, warning, CTAs |
| Blue | #42b1ff | Technical, intelligence, trust, links |
| Green | #44b574 | Success, solution, proof |
| Purple | #c985e5 | Premium, financial, analytical |

```css
/* Brand Accent Colors — pick ONE per design */
:root {
  --clr-orange: #FF8B58;
  --clr-blue: #42b1ff;
  --clr-green: #44b574;
  --clr-purple: #c985e5;
}
```

## Neutrals (weight: 90)

| Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Black | #000000 | Social backgrounds (pure black) |
| Near-black | #050505 | Website backgrounds |
| Section bg | #111 / #161616 | Section fills |
| White | #ffffff | Primary text |
| Warm white | #f5f0e8 | Body text (warm off-white) |
| Secondary | #888888 | Supporting text |

### RGBA Overlay Patterns

| Value | Usage | Weight |
|-------|-------|--------|
| `rgba(255,255,255,0.45)` | Body copy | 85 |
| `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` | Dimmed/secondary | 80 |
| `rgba(255,255,255,0.03)` | Card backgrounds | 80 |
| `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` | Card borders | 75 |
| `#1a1a1a` / `#222` | Borders/dividers | 75 |

```css
/* Neutrals — backgrounds, text, overlays */
:root {
  --clr-bg: #000;             /* Social: pure black */
  --clr-bg-page: #050505;     /* Website: near-black */
  --clr-bg-section: #111;     /* Section fills */
  --clr-text: #ffffff;        /* Primary text */
  --clr-text-warm: #f5f0e8;   /* Body text (warm) */
  --clr-text-secondary: #888; /* Supporting text */
  --clr-border: #1a1a1a;      /* Borders, dividers */
}
.body-copy    { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.45); }  /* Weight: 85 */
.dimmed       { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); }  /* Weight: 80 */
.card         { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }
```

brand_patterns.label: Layout Archetypes
brand_patterns.content:
6 proven layout types for social posts. Each validated through 10+ iterations.

## A. Full-Bleed Headline (weight: 85)

**Best for:** Brand statements, problem declarations, emotional hooks

**Structure:**
1. HEADLINE (82-100px) — fills top portion, dominates visual weight
2. FLFont tagline — accent color
3. Body (1-2 sentences max) — supporting text
4. Footer — standard brand footer
5. Brushstrokes: 2

## B. Headline + Diagram Card (weight: 80)

**Best for:** Technical differentiators, before/after, process explanations

**Structure:**
1. HEADLINE (82px) — top section
2. Diagram / Terminal / Flow — card with `rgba(255,255,255,0.03)` bg and `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` border
3. Footer

## C. Giant Stat Hero (weight: 80)

**Best for:** Proof points, reframes, "the number you're not tracking"

**Structure:**
1. Giant stat number (120px+) — center, largest element
2. Headline below stat — contextualizes the number
3. Body context — brief explanation
4. Circle: around stat — emphasis element
5. Footer

## D. Pull Quote / Manifesto (weight: 80)

**Best for:** Company voice, emotional storytelling, thought leadership

**Structure:**
1. Large opening quote mark (decorative, ~10% opacity)
2. Quote text (50-52px) — the core message
3. FLFont attribution — speaker/source
4. Footer

## E. Two-Column — LinkedIn only (weight: 75)

**Best for:** Educational content, comparisons. 1200x627 landscape ONLY.

**Structure:**
- Left column (55%): Headline + body text
- Right column (45%): Visual element (diagram, mockup, or illustration)
- Footer

## F. Centered Manifesto (weight: 75)

**Best for:** Mission statements, campaign anchors. Heavy brush framing both sides.

**Structure:**
1. Ghost watermark text (~4% opacity, massive, fills background)
2. Centered headline — on top of watermark
3. Centered body — supporting text
4. Footer
5. Brushstrokes: heavy framing on both sides

## Selection Guide

| Archetype | When to Use | Accent |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| Full-Bleed Headline | Default for most pain/problem posts | Orange |
| Headline + Diagram | Technical or process content | Blue |
| Giant Stat Hero | Data-driven proof points | Green |
| Pull Quote / Manifesto | Thought leadership, storytelling | Any |
| Two-Column | LinkedIn landscape educational | Blue |
| Centered Manifesto | Campaign anchors, mission | Purple |

brand_patterns.label: Visual Compositor Contract
brand_patterns.content:
# Visual Compositor Contract — Fluid Social Posts

These rules define what makes a Fluid social post look "designed" rather than a web page.

## Canvas
- Fixed canvas with `overflow: hidden` — this is a poster, not a web page
- No scrolling. Content must fit within the fixed dimensions.

## Required Visual Layers (back to front)
1. **Background** — solid color or gradient, extends to all edges
2. **Texture/Brushstroke** — at least one decorative element with `mix-blend-mode: screen` and `opacity: 0.10–0.25`, positioned partially off-canvas (edge-bleed)
3. **Content** — headline, subtext, positioned via `position: absolute` (not flexbox/grid)
4. **Footer** — Fluid logo + We-Commerce wordmark left, Fluid dots right

## Typography Scale
- Headline must be 5–8x larger than body text (e.g., headline 72–120px, body 14–18px)
- Use only `flfontbold` for brand taglines and `NeueHaas` (Inter) for all other text
- Maximum 2 font families per post

## Positioning
- All major elements positioned with `position: absolute` and pixel values
- Content intentionally inset from edges (40–80px padding from canvas border)
- Background extends to edges; content does not

## Required Elements
- Logo (Fluid logo from brand assets)
- At least one tagline in flfontbold
- At least one decorative element (brushstroke or circle sketch)
- At least one `mix-blend-mode` or CSS `filter` effect

## Color
- Limited palette: one accent color + black (#000) + white (#fff) + opacity variants
- Accent colors: orange (#FF6B35) for pain, blue (#4A90D9) for trust, green (#7BC67E) for proof, purple (#9B59B6) for premium
- Use `rgba()` for opacity variants, not separate color definitions

## Anti-Patterns (reframed as positive constraints)
- Use `position: absolute` for layout, not CSS Grid or Flexbox
- Use pixel dimensions matching the target canvas (1080x1080 for Instagram)
- All @font-face declarations must use URLs from list_brand_assets, never base64
- All image/texture references must use URLs from list_brand_assets, never base64

template_design_rules.label: Social Media — General Rules
template_design_rules.content: General rules about social media posts — canvas dimensions, the compositor layer model, footer requirements, typography scale ratio. Cross-reference the Visual Compositor Contract for full details. These rules apply to ALL social media posts regardless of platform.

template_design_rules.label: Instagram Brand Guidelines
template_design_rules.content: Instagram-specific rules — 1080x1080px square canvas, high-contrast text for mobile viewing, bold headline-forward compositions, touch-friendly CTA placement (bottom third).

template_design_rules.label: LinkedIn Brand Guidelines
template_design_rules.content: LinkedIn-specific rules — 1200x627px landscape canvas, professional but bold tone, slightly more restrained brushstroke usage, text-heavy compositions acceptable (professional audience reads more).

template_design_rules.label: Problem-First Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Problem-First — headline dominates canvas (60%+ of visual weight). Pain-point text in large flfontbold. Accent: orange (#FF6B35). Dark background with brushstroke texture bleeding off top-right edge. Subtext small and secondary.

template_design_rules.label: Quote/Testimonial Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Quote/Testimonial — centered quote in large flfontbold with circle sketch accent behind key word. Attribution smaller below. Brushstroke texture subtle, background usually dark.

template_design_rules.label: Stat Proof Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Stat Proof — giant number (120px+) as focal point. Supporting text frames the stat's meaning. Green (#7BC67E) accent for proof/evidence. Minimal decoration — the number IS the decoration.

template_design_rules.label: App Highlight Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: App Highlight — product feature showcase. Blue (#4A90D9) accent for trust. Screenshot or UI element as visual anchor. Text describes the feature benefit, not the feature itself.

template_design_rules.label: Manifesto Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Manifesto — brand philosophy statement. Large flfontbold text fills most of canvas. Minimal decoration — text IS the design. Purple (#9B59B6) accent for premium/aspirational.

template_design_rules.label: Partner Alert Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Partner Alert — partnership announcement. Dual-brand friendly layout. Blue (#4A90D9) accent. Logo placement prominent. Announcement-style headline.

template_design_rules.label: Feature Spotlight Design Notes
template_design_rules.content: Feature Spotlight — product capability deep-dive. Blue (#4A90D9) accent for trust/technology. More technical copy acceptable. Visual balance between text and illustrative element.
